name: Morning Briefing
description: A one-screen weekday briefing — weather, today's calendar with prep, urgent unread messages, and the one thing to tackle first.
long_description: Skip the morning news scroll. This skill produces a plain-markdown briefing (under 300 words, readable in 30 seconds) that pulls weather, today's calendar with any prep needed, urgent messages from the last 12 hours, and flags the one thing to tackle first. Includes a daily vocabulary pull from Word Genius for good measure. Meant to run every weekday at 7:30am so your day starts without opening five apps.
skill_md_preview:
---
name: briefing
description: Generate a one-screen weekday morning briefing — weather, calendar, urgent messages, top priority.
---

# Morning Briefing

Give me a one-screen morning briefing I can read in 30 seconds before my day starts.

## Output

- Under 300 words. No greeting, no signoff.
- **Weather** — framed so I know how to dress.
- **Calendar** — today, including prep I'll need.
- **Urgent messages** — last 12 hours, unreplied.
- **Top priority** — the one thing to tackle first.
- **Word of the day** — from Word Genius email.

## Guardrails

- Never include private/sensitive channels.
- Work email only — no personal.
- If a source is unavailable, say so. Never fabricate.

name: Coffee Chat Prep
description: Turn a name + LinkedIn URL into a 1-page brief with talking points, recent news, and smart questions.
long_description: Paste a classmate's or alum's LinkedIn URL and this skill produces a one-page coffee chat brief: who they are, what they're working on, recent company news, two personal-angle talking points, and five questions to ask. Built for GSB students prepping for back-to-back chats during treks and recruiting weeks.
skill_md_preview:
---
name: coffee-chat-prep
description: One-page coffee chat brief from a LinkedIn URL
---

# Coffee Chat Prep

When the user gives you a LinkedIn URL or a name + company, produce a one-page brief with:

1. **Who they are** — current role, previous roles, school.
2. **What they're working on** — recent posts, company news from the last 60 days.
3. **Two personal-angle hooks** — something specific, not generic.
4. **Five smart questions** — things an associate actually wants to be asked.

Never fabricate. If a data source isn't available, say so.

name: Case Prep Partner
description: A case interviewer on demand — picks a case type, probes for structure, and gives honest feedback.
long_description: Your silent case prep partner. Choose market sizing, profitability, or M&A — the skill picks a realistic case, pushes back when your structure is shaky, asks for math, and ends with a scorecard (framework, math, synthesis, communication) plus one thing to work on. No sugarcoating.
skill_md_preview:
---
name: case-prep-partner
description: Mock case interviewer for consulting recruiting
---

# Case Prep Partner

Act as a senior consultant running a 30-minute case. Start by asking the user which case type they want (market sizing, profitability, M&A, growth). Pick a realistic prompt. Push for a clean structure before letting them move on. Make them do the math out loud. End with:

- Framework score (1-5)
- Math score (1-5)
- Synthesis score (1-5)
- One concrete thing to practice

name: Weekly Homework Planner
description: Reads your syllabus PDFs and builds a Monday-to-Sunday study plan that fits around class and clubs.
long_description: Drop in your syllabi for the quarter. The planner extracts every assignment, reading, and due date, then lays out a realistic week that respects your calendar (no studying during Section, Arbuckle dinners, or the Friday slam). Re-runs weekly so the plan stays fresh.
skill_md_preview:
---
name: weekly-homework-planner
description: Build a realistic weekly study plan from syllabi + calendar
---

# Weekly Homework Planner

Inputs:
- Syllabus PDFs in ./syllabi/
- User's Google Calendar for the next 7 days

Output: a markdown plan with one section per day. For each day, list the 1-3 most important tasks given deadlines, each with a realistic time block that does not overlap meetings or classes.

Rules:
- Never schedule work between 6pm Friday and 6pm Saturday.
- Always leave one fully open evening per week.
- Flag anything due in <48h at the top of the plan.

name: Section Social Planner
description: Picks a venue, drafts the invite, and handles the poll for your next section outing.
long_description: Tell it the vibe (hike, dinner, dive bar, beach day) and a rough budget. The skill finds three Peninsula/SF options, drafts a short invite message with time suggestions, and spins up a poll you can paste into WhatsApp or iMessage. Remembers what your section has done before so you don't do trivia three weeks in a row.
skill_md_preview:
---
name: section-social-planner
description: Plan the next section outing end-to-end
---

# Section Social Planner

When the user describes the vibe they want (e.g. 'low-key dinner outside, ~$40pp'), do three things:

1. Suggest three Peninsula or SF venues that fit (name, neighborhood, price, why).
2. Draft a 2-sentence invite message with 2-3 time options.
3. Produce a poll-ready block (`Option A / Option B / Option C`) that pastes cleanly into WhatsApp.

Never suggest a venue the section has been to in the last 30 days.
